content:
{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":"Policy Playground — Interactive OPA Rule Sandbox with Shareable Scenarios","description":"Expose the marketplace's Rego policy engine (marketplace.rego already exists) through a browser-based sandbox where operators, researchers, and auditors can paste custom task definitions, tweak policy parameters, and instantly see what the enforcement engine allows, denies, or flags. Shareable scenario links let teams debate edge cases publicly. Pre-loaded 'classic disputes' from the Task Autopsy feed serve as ready-made test cases. This transforms an internal compliance tool into a community knowledge hub and developer onboarding accelerator.","viralPotential":"Security researchers share 'I found a policy bypass scenario' write-ups linking directly to the sandbox. Bot builders share their custom policy configs as credibility signals. Becomes the canonical reference for 'how does Clawbot actually enforce rules' — drives organic SEO and developer community traffic.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Thin API wrapper around OPA evaluation endpoint, scenario serialization to URL-safe base64 for shareable links, 5-10 pre-loaded canonical scenarios drawn from real dispute categories. Week 2: Monaco-editor-based UI with Rego syntax highlighting, split-pane input/output, diff view showing which policy clauses fired, and a 'Publish Scenario' flow that adds it to a public community gallery.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Sandbox Editor: Three-pane layout — Policy (Rego, read-only base + editable override layer) on the left, Task Input JSON in the center, Evaluation Result (allow/deny + triggered rules highlighted in yellow) on the right, with a 'Share Scenario' button generating a permalink","Screen 2 — Scenario Gallery: Card grid of community-submitted and official scenarios tagged by category (escrow, identity, dispute, rate-limit), sortable by 'most shared' and 'most surprising outcome', each card showing a one-line description and the policy verdict badge","Screen 3 — Policy Diff View: Side-by-side comparison of base marketplace policy vs. a proposed custom override, with line-level annotations showing which task types are newly allowed, newly denied, or unchanged — exportable as a PDF audit report"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8.5},{"title":"Dispute Storm — Crowdsourced Dispute Resolution with Jury Mechanics","description":"When a task dispute is filed, a randomly selected jury of verified marketplace participants (bots and humans) reviews anonymized evidence bundles and votes on resolution. Jurors earn reputation points and small fee splits for accurate verdicts (aligned with final outcomes). Leverages the existing dispute target validation and evidence filtering infrastructure already hardened in the codebase. The twist: verdicts are public, jurors are revealed post-resolution, and dissenting opinions are published — creating a transparent, self-regulating trust layer.","viralPotential":"Jurors share their 'I ruled on this wild bot dispute' badges. Dramatic disputes become spectator events. Builders and bot operators cite 'jury-tested' resolution history as a trust signal when marketing their bots externally. Creates appointment viewing around high-stakes disputes.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Dispute queue API endpoint that packages anonymized evidence bundles + juror selection from verified participant pool (3-5 jurors per dispute). Week 2: Simple voting UI (Guilty/Not Guilty + confidence slider), verdict aggregation logic, juror reputation delta writes, and public verdict feed endpoint. Reuse existing escrow/ledger for juror micro-rewards.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Jury Summons: Notification card showing 'You've been selected as juror #3 for Dispute #4421' with anonymized task summary, evidence attachments, and a countdown timer before the case closes","Screen 2 — Evidence Room: Split-panel view with claimant evidence on the left, respondent evidence on the right, verdict buttons (Uphold / Reject / Partial) at the bottom plus a free-text reasoning field","Screen 3 — Public Verdict Feed: Chronological list of resolved disputes showing task type, jury split (e.g. 4-1), final ruling, and collapsed dissenting opinion — filterable by task category and ruling type"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":7.5},{"title":"Bot Draft — Scheduled Auction Windows for High-Demand Task Slots","description":"Certain recurring or high-value task types (e.g. data scraping windows, API polling jobs, time-sensitive automations) are auctioned in scheduled 10-minute draft windows rather than first-come-first-served. Bots pre-register intent, escrow is locked speculatively during the window, and the highest-trust-adjusted bid wins the lease. Trust score (from Moltbook identity) acts as a bid multiplier — a bot with 2x reputation needs to bid less than a newcomer to win. This surfaces the existing lease timing and escrow infrastructure in a competitive, appointment-driven format.","viralPotential":"Bot operators post 'We won the 9AM scraping slot for the 6th week straight' flex posts. Losing bots get a public 'outbid by' attribution they can dispute or train against. Draft schedules get shared like sports fixtures. Creates a recurring reason to return to the marketplace daily.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Draft schedule config (admin-defined windows per task category), pre-registration endpoint, speculative escrow lock during window using existing ledger primitives. Week 2: Bid submission WebSocket feed (already authenticated per BUG-CRIT-001 fix), trust-adjusted scoring formula, winner announcement + loser escrow release, and a live draft-room UI.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Draft Calendar: Weekly grid showing upcoming auction windows per task category, with a 'Register Intent' button per slot and a live participant count badge counting down to each window open","Screen 2 — Live Draft Room: Real-time bid ticker showing anonymized competing bots, your current trust-adjusted standing, a bid amount input with a projected win-probability bar that updates as others bid, and a 60-second final-call countdown","Screen 3 — Draft Results: Post-auction summary card showing winner (bot handle + trust tier), clearing price, your finishing rank, and a one-click 'Set Alert for Next Window' CTA"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":7.5}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"Clawbot-marketplace","description":"Production-oriented implementation scaffold for a public verified Clawbot task marketplace with:","techStack":["TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["e9f0059 fix(security+hardening): BUG-HIGH-001 CORS, BUG-MED-001 timing-safe lease, BUG-MED-002 evidence filtering, BUG-HIGH-003 dispute target validation, BUG-MED-003 policy enforcement — 314 tests passing","47972df fix(api): authenticate WebSocket endpoints (BUG-CRIT-001)","a227347 feat(security+integrity): TASK-HARD-007/008/010 + TASK-FEAT-001/006 + fix pre-existing test failures","c5e9fea arch: comprehensive bug inventory — 14 untracked bugs + fix task definitions","bc3fb14 test(api): comprehensive endpoint and integration test coverage","4e16581 arch: comprehensive architecture deep-dive — Moltbook, escrow, state machines, security","7d4654b test(escrow): add comprehensive escrow and ledger test coverage (TASK-HARD-006)","00a1ba0 init: clawbot marketplace v1 alpha baseline"],"fileStructure":"./infra/deployments.log

{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":""Jugaad Mode" — Budget-Capped Auto-Router","description":"Users set a monthly ₹ budget and a task type (chat, summarization, code, image), and Jugaad Mode automatically routes each API call to the cheapest model that meets a minimum quality threshold. If GPT-4o is overkill for a simple FAQ bot, it silently downgrades to Mistral or Llama — saving real money. A live ticker shows 'You saved ₹847 this month via Jugaad Mode.' The name is intentionally desi and shareable — it frames frugal AI as clever, not cheap.","viralPotential":"The ₹ savings ticker is inherently screenshot-worthy. Indian devs and startup founders will brag about cost savings on Twitter/X and LinkedIn. 'Jugaad Mode saved my startup ₹12,000 this month' is a tweet that writes itself. The cheeky brand name travels well in Indian tech circles.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Build a rule-based routing config UI — user picks task type + monthly ₹ cap, system maps to a priority-ordered model list. Week 2: Wrap existing inference API calls with a thin router middleware that checks cost-per-token against cap, selects cheapest eligible model, logs actual vs. would-have-spent cost, and surfaces the savings counter on the dashboard.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Jugaad Mode Setup Card: Toggle switch labeled 'Enable Jugaad Mode', a ₹ monthly budget slider (₹500–₹50,000), task-type selector (Chat / Code / Summarize / Vision), and a preview table showing 'If budget tight → Llama 3.1 8B | If budget ok → Mistral Large | Default → GPT-4o Mini'.","Screen 2 — Savings Dashboard Widget: A prominent green card on the main dashboard showing '₹ Saved This Month: ₹2,340', a sparkline of daily savings, and a breakdown table: 'Calls rerouted: 1,204 | Avg quality score maintained: 91% | Top swap: GPT-4o → Mistral Large (₹1,100 saved)'."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8.666666666666666},{"title":""Chai Break" Code Review Bot — GitHub PR Summarizer","description":"A one-click GitHub App integration where developers connect their repo and AI Bazaar auto-posts a plain-English (or Hinglish) PR summary as a comment every time a pull request is opened. The summary includes: what changed, potential bugs spotted, and estimated complexity. Powered by the cheapest capable model on the platform, billed per-PR at a flat ₹2–5 rate shown upfront. The 'Chai Break' framing means: 'while you're making chai, AI already read the PR.'","viralPotential":"Every PR comment has a footer: 'Summarized by AI Bazaar Chai Break — ₹3.20 | Try free →'. Every engineer on the team sees it. Every open-source repo using it becomes a passive billboard. GitHub is the highest-density network for developer word-of-mouth in India's startup ecosystem.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Build a minimal GitHub OAuth App that listens to pull_request.opened webhooks, extracts the diff, and sends it to the AI Bazaar inference API with a fixed summarization prompt. Week 2: Build a 3-screen onboarding UI (connect GitHub → select repos → choose language EN/HI/Hinglish), add per-PR cost logging, and render the branded comment with cost breakdown footer.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Connect & Configure: GitHub OAuth button, repo multi-select checklist, language toggle (English / Hindi / Hinglish), and a live cost estimator: 'Avg PR size on your repos ≈ 340 lines → estimated ₹3–4 per PR'.","Screen 2 — PR Comment Preview (rendered in GitHub): A collapsible comment block with 🍵 Chai Break Summary header, three sections — 'What changed' (2–3 bullet points), 'Watch out for' (flagged logic), 'Complexity: Medium' — and a footer showing '₹3.20 via AI Bazaar | Powered by Mistral Large'.","Screen 3 — Usage Feed in Dashboard: A reverse-chronological list of PRs summarized, each row showing repo name, PR title, model used, cost, and a 'View Comment' deep-link back to GitHub."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8},{"title":""Dukaan AI Kit" — Instant AI Feature Pack for Indian SMB Websites","description":"A one-page wizard that lets non-technical Indian small business owners (kirana stores, tutors, clinics, travel agents) embed three pre-built AI widgets into any website via a single script tag: (1) a WhatsApp-style FAQ chatbot trained on their uploaded PDF/menu, (2) a product/service description generator in their regional language, and (3) an auto-reply suggestion tool for common customer queries. All billed in ₹ with a free tier of 500 queries/month. No coding, no API keys — just paste one line.","viralPotential":"The embed script tag has a subtle 'Powered by AI Bazaar' badge that links back to the platform. Every SMB website using it becomes a referral node. Business owners in WhatsApp groups will share 'I added AI to my shop website for ₹0' — that message format explodes in Tier-2/3 city entrepreneur groups, which are massive and underserved by current AI tools.","mvpScope":"Week 1: Build the onboarding wizard (business type selector → PDF/text upload → language picker → generate embed snippet). Implement a single chatbot widget backed by a RAG-lite prompt (stuff the uploaded text into context, use cheapest model). Week 2: Add the description generator tab (input: product name + 3 keywords → output: 3 language variants), basic query count metering, and the free-tier cap with an upgrade nudge at 400/500 queries.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Dukaan Setup Wizard (3 steps): Step 1: Business type card grid (🛒 Retail / 📚 Tutor / 🏥 Clinic / ✈️ Travel / Other). Step 2: Upload menu/catalogue/FAQ as PDF or paste text, with a language selector (12 Indian languages listed). Step 3: Copy embed snippet with a one-line script tag and a live preview iframe showing the chat widget.","Screen 2 — Live Widget Preview: A mobile-frame mockup showing the chat widget with the business's name, a sample FAQ exchange in the selected language, and the 'Powered by AI Bazaar' badge at the bottom.","Screen 3 — Dukaan Dashboard: Query usage bar (423 / 500 free queries used), a table of recent questions asked by customers with the AI's answers, a 'Retrain' button to re-upload an updated PDF, and an upgrade CTA: 'Unlock unlimited queries for ₹199/month'."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":7.333333333333333}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"AI-Bazaar","description":"AI Bazaar is an India-first AI marketplace and inference platform.","techStack":["Next.js","React","Tailwind CSS","TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["bbe7d09 fix: prevent API_KEY_HASH_SALT throw during Vercel build-time page collection","5e2a81a chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, all tests passing","f2119f2 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","afa4b68 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","34eb7b5 docs: architect session 3 — ARCHITECTURE.md rewrite, ADR-013, TASKS.md update","b39483c fix: allow API_KEY_HASH_SALT fallback during Next.js build phase","b24f986 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","433b025 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","c104300 chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, 93 test files (1347 tests) passing, zero TS errors","8a6636a feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing","c8339c2 feat: AI Bazaar project 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","f0ae4a9 chore: supervision cycle — project 100% complete, 88/88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing","4413e5b feat: AI Bazaar complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","3d1f935 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347/1347 tests pass, build fully clean","d0d0f2b chore: supervision cycle — all 88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing, build clean","1bd29e8 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347 tests pass, all agents completed, project 100% complete","7f116ce feat: AI Bazaar 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","2b5fc6f chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing, 0 lint errors, 0 type errors","f59a739 feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","d554772 feat: AI Bazaar v1.0 — all 88 tasks complete, 1347 tests passing"],"fileStructure":"./.vercel/project.json
